goal: Layer CRUD + bi-temporal queries + hot-staging writes on top of the existing §12.1 `knowledge_graph.rs` schema (landed by WO-0011) so that P1-W4-F02 (CRUD + bi-temporal) and P1-W4-F08 (hot staging) flip to passes=true. **Second fresh restart** — supersedes the abandoned WO-0012 (zero commits, zero .rs diff; RCA at `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md`) AND the abandoned WO-0020 (one commit `fa8b6af` adding chrono workspace dep, then session-abandonment; no ready-for-review marker, no subsequent implementation). Both prior attempts failed with NO technical blocker per the WO-0012 RCA — the environment is healthy, `cargo build --workspace` is green on `main`, the spec is unambiguous, and `execute_in_transaction` is already in place at `knowledge_graph.rs:430-440`. Unblocks the downstream Week-4 cascade: P1-W4-F03 (symbol resolution), P1-W4-F04 (extraction pipeline), P1-W4-F10 (get_conventions), and transitively P1-W4-F05 / P1-W4-F09 / P1-W5-F02 / P1-W5-F09.
plan_summary: Extend `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` with typed `Entity` / `Relation` / `HotObservation` structs plus CRUD + bi-temporal + hot-staging helpers. Every writer routes through the existing `execute_in_transaction` chokepoint (which already uses `TransactionBehavior::Immediate`). INIT_SQL stays byte-for-byte frozen — the entities/relations/hot_observations/hot_convention_signals/hot_architecture_deltas tables + their UNIQUE/foreign-key constraints are already in place from WO-0011 (verified at `knowledge_graph.rs:110-216`). All tests live at module root as `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_<name>() { ... }` (DEC-0005 / WO-0006-WO-0007 selector-mismatch avoidance) mirroring the existing `test_schema_creation` at line 472-474 AND the five `quality_pipeline::test_*` landed by WO-0016 AND the seven `call_hierarchy::test_*` landed by WO-0023 (the verified module-root-flat precedent set). Timestamps use `chrono::DateTime<Utc>::to_rfc3339()` so `t_valid_from`/`t_valid_to` string-comparisons in SQLite are lexicographically correct. The `upsert_entity` flow uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) DO UPDATE SET t_last_verified = datetime('now'), access_count = access_count + 1 RETURNING id` so the caller gets the inserted-or-updated row id in a single round-trip. `stage_hot_observation` / `stage_hot_convention_signal` / `stage_hot_architecture_delta` writers route through `execute_in_transaction` to satisfy the master-plan §11 line 1117 BEGIN IMMEDIATE invariant. A new `WalCheckpointMode { Passive, Truncate }` enum + `checkpoint_wal` helper wraps `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(<MODE>)` for the configurable interval (F08). Workspace root `Cargo.toml` gains `chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"] }` under `[workspace.dependencies]` (matches how `uuid` is organised at line 70); `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml` pulls it via `chrono = { workspace = true }`. No schema DDL change. No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection` — every test opens a real tempfile-backed SQLite db via `tempfile::TempDir::new()` + `KnowledgeGraph::open(...)`. Branch starts from a CLEAN `main` (do NOT rebase/cherry-pick from `feat/WO-0012-*` or `feat/WO-0020-*`).
scope_in: Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"] }` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (default-features OFF avoids the `oldtime` / `time 0.1` transitive chain that emits clippy-deny warnings on some rustc versions — see WO-0012 RCA §Risk point 5)
crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml — add `chrono = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct Entity` with fields (exact order): `pub id: Option<i64>` (None before insert, Some(rowid) after), `pub kind: String`, `pub name: String`, `pub qualified_name: Option<String>`, `pub file_path: String`, `pub start_line: Option<i64>`, `pub end_line: Option<i64>`, `pub signature: Option<String>`, `pub doc_comment: Option<String>`, `pub language: Option<String>`, `pub t_valid_from: Option<String>` (RFC-3339), `pub t_valid_to: Option<String>` (RFC-3339), `pub importance: f64`, `pub source_tool: Option<String>`, `pub source_hash: Option<String>` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct Relation` with fields: `pub id: Option<i64>`, `pub source_id: i64`, `pub target_id: i64`, `pub kind: String`, `pub weight: f64`, `pub t_valid_from: Option<String>`, `pub t_valid_to: Option<String>`, `pub source_tool: Option<String>`, `pub source_evidence: Option<String>`, `pub confidence: f64` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct HotObservation` with fields: `pub raw_text: String`, `pub session_id: Option<String>`, `pub related_file: Option<String>`, `pub related_symbol: Option<String>` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `pub enum WalCheckpointMode { Passive, Truncate }` with `impl WalCheckpointMode { const fn as_sql(self) -> &'static str { match self { Self::Passive => "PASSIVE", Self::Truncate => "TRUNCATE" } } }` — derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::upsert_entity(&mut self, entity: &Entity) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO entities (kind, name, qualified_name, file_path, start_line, end_line, signature, doc_comment, language, t_valid_from, t_valid_to, importance, source_tool, source_hash) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) DO UPDATE SET t_last_verified = datetime('now'), access_count = access_count + 1 RETURNING id` and returns the resulting rowid. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self, entity), fields(name=%entity.name))]` per master-plan §15.2 (span name `ucil.core.kg.upsert_entity`)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_by_qualified_name(&self, qualified_name: &str, file_path: Option<&str>) -> Result<Option<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only via `self.conn.prepare(...)?.query_row(...)`; returns the most recent row (`ORDER BY t_ingested_at DESC LIMIT 1`)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::list_entities_by_file(&self, file_path: &str) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only; returns rows ordered by `start_line` ascending (so callers get document-order)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_as_of(&self, qualified_name: &str, at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>) -> Result<Option<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — bi-temporal read: `WHERE qualified_name = ?1 AND t_valid_from <= ?2 AND (t_valid_to IS NULL OR t_valid_to > ?2) ORDER BY t_valid_from DESC LIMIT 1`. The `at` parameter is encoded via `at.to_rfc3339()` so the string comparison is lexicographic. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self))]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::upsert_relation(&mut self, relation: &Relation) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO relations (source_id, target_id, kind, weight, t_valid_from, t_valid_to, source_tool, source_evidence, confidence) VALUES (...) RETURNING id` (no ON CONFLICT clause — `relations` has no UNIQUE constraint in §12.1)

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::list_relations_by_source(&self, source_id: i64) -> Result<Vec<Relation>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only; returns all rows with the given `source_id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_observation(&mut self, observation: &HotObservation) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction` (BEGIN IMMEDIATE); issues `INSERT INTO hot_observations (raw_text, session_id, related_file, related_symbol) VALUES (...) RETURNING id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_convention_signal(&mut self, pattern_hash: &str, file_path: &str, example_snippet: Option<&str>) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO hot_convention_signals (pattern_hash, file_path, example_snippet) VALUES (...) RETURNING id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_architecture_delta(&mut self, change_type: &str, file_path: &str, details: Option<&str>) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; issues `INSERT INTO hot_architecture_deltas (change_type, file_path, details) VALUES (...) RETURNING id`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::checkpoint_wal(&self, mode: WalCheckpointMode) -> Result<(i64, i64, i64), KnowledgeGraphError>` — wraps `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(<MODE>)`; returns `(busy, log_frames, checkpointed)` tuple as rusqlite reports it. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self))]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — extend `KnowledgeGraphError` with the new variants the CRUD paths can surface: `#[error("invalid timestamp: {0}")] InvalidTimestamp(String)` for future use, AND no other new variants (rusqlite errors flow through the existing `#[from] rusqlite::Error` arm). `#[non_exhaustive]` is already on the enum
crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs — extend the existing re-export line so it emits `pub use knowledge_graph::{Entity, HotObservation, KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError, Relation, WalCheckpointMode};`

Module-root tests in `knowledge_graph.rs` — ALL at module level under `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` (NOT inside `mod tests { }`, per DEC-0005 — the `test_schema_creation` precedent at line 472-474 is the shape; `quality_pipeline::test_*` and `call_hierarchy::test_*` are the most recent verified precedents). Required tests (exact names — `test_hot_staging_writes` is the **frozen F08 selector**):
* `test_upsert_and_get_entity` — inserts an `Entity`, calls `get_entity_by_qualified_name`, asserts all fields round-trip (including `Option<String>` fields)

* `test_bi_temporal_as_of` — inserts three `Entity` rows for the same `qualified_name` with staggered `t_valid_from` and `t_valid_to`; queries `get_entity_as_of` at a timestamp between row-2 and row-3; asserts row-2 is returned; queries at a timestamp after row-3; asserts row-3 is returned
* `test_list_entities_by_file` — inserts 3 `Entity` rows for the same `file_path` with ascending `start_line`; calls `list_entities_by_file`; asserts document-order return
* `test_upsert_relation_and_list` — inserts 2 `Entity` rows, then a `Relation` between them; calls `list_relations_by_source`; asserts the single relation is returned with all fields intact

* `test_entity_unique_constraint_updates` — inserts the same `(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from)` twice; asserts the second call returns the same `id` AND increments `access_count` (read back from the `entities` table) AND updates `t_last_verified`

* `test_hot_staging_writes` — **FROZEN F08 SELECTOR**. Calls `stage_hot_observation`, `stage_hot_convention_signal`, `stage_hot_architecture_delta` each once; reads each table back via `self.conn.query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hot_observations", ...)` (and similar for the other two); asserts each count is 1. Also asserts the call went through a BEGIN IMMEDIATE txn by checking the returned row's id is positive (RETURNING id succeeded)

* `test_wal_checkpoint_truncates` — writes ≥ 10 rows to `hot_observations` to grow the WAL; calls `checkpoint_wal(WalCheckpointMode::Truncate)`; asserts the returned `(busy, log_frames, checkpointed)` tuple has `busy == 0` and `log_frames >= 0` (exact numbers are OS-specific; the point is the call succeeds and returns a tuple)

Rustdoc on every new public item: `Entity`, `Relation`, `HotObservation`, `WalCheckpointMode`, and each new `KnowledgeGraph::*` method — cite master-plan §12.1 (schema), §11 line 1117 (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), §15.2 (tracing spans). Do NOT add new module-level rustdoc — the existing rustdoc block at lines 1-42 already covers the module; extend it only if pedantic requires it.

No edits to `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md` — it is the RCA record used as context for this retry; keep it as historical record

No rebase / cherry-pick from `feat/WO-0012-kg-crud-and-hot-staging` or `feat/WO-0020-kg-crud-and-hot-staging` — both branches are stale dead-ends. Start the `feat/WO-0024-*` branch fresh from `origin/main`. (The chrono dep commit `fa8b6af` on WO-0020 is isolated and unreviewed; redo it as commit 1 of WO-0024 so the branch is self-contained and reviewable.)

master-plan:§12.1 lines 1127-1318 (§12.1 schema DDL — the 16 tables + indexes — already landed in INIT_SQL by WO-0011; DO NOT edit)

master-plan:§15.2 (tracing span naming convention: `ucil.<layer>.<op>` — span names `ucil.core.kg.upsert_entity`, `ucil.core.kg.get_entity_as_of`, `ucil.core.kg.stage_hot_observation`, `ucil.core.kg.checkpoint_wal`)
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 4 lines 1747-1754 (CRUD + bi-temporal + hot-staging scope — `Week 4 — SQLite knowledge graph + basic queries`)
master-plan:§9.1 data layout (`.ucil/shared/knowledge.db` — the target path, already realised by `KnowledgeGraph::open`)

work-order:WO-0012-kg-crud-and-hot-staging (ABANDONED 1ST PREDECESSOR — same features, rejected for executor session-abandonment, not content. Branch `feat/WO-0012-*` local-only, never pushed. RCA at `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md` documents 'no technical blocker' with 95% confidence)

work-order:WO-0020-kg-crud-and-hot-staging (ABANDONED 2ND PREDECESSOR — same features. Branch `feat/WO-0020-*` has ONE commit `fa8b6af` adding chrono workspace dep, then session-abandonment. No ready-for-review marker, no critic report, no verification. WO-0024 redoes the chrono setup as commit 1 so the branch is self-contained.)

work-order:WO-0021-daemon-lifecycle-and-session-state (RECENT FRESH-RESTART SUCCESS — superseded abandoned WO-0008; PASSED on first verifier cycle. The pattern: fresh branch, clear supersedes context, follow the plan)
work-order:WO-0022-storage-layout-and-crash-recovery (RECENT FRESH-RESTART SUCCESS — superseded abandoned WO-0006; PASSED on first verifier cycle. Module-root-flat tests + frozen selector discipline)

work-order:WO-0023-lsp-call-and-type-hierarchy-feed (RECENT SUCCESS — mirrored WO-0016's pattern exactly; 7 module-root tests; PASSED on first verifier cycle. The exemplar of 'copy the working template' discipline)
rca:ucil-build/verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md (full remediation plan — commit sequence, non-negotiable invariants, and known pitfalls. The executor MUST read this before starting)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror; pedantic+nursery; tracing; no unwrap outside #[cfg(test)]; no unsafe without ADR; edition 2021)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariant 1: no mocks of SQLite; invariant 8: SQLite opens in WAL mode with PRAGMA busy_timeout = 10000 — already enforced in WO-0011)
lessons_applied: **TWO prior abandonments on these exact features (WO-0012 + WO-0020) — session-abandonment is the failure mode, NOT a technical blocker.** The WO-0012 RCA (`verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md`) concluded with 95% confidence: `cargo build --workspace` is green on `main`, the spec is unambiguous, `execute_in_transaction` is already in place. The executor MUST treat this as a discipline-not-difficulty problem. The WO-0021/0022/0023 recent successes prove that when the executor simply commits + pushes incrementally, the plan lands on first verifier cycle.

**Anti-abandonment safeguard** — before ending ANY session on this WO, the executor MUST verify: (a) `git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline` shows ≥ 6 commits on the feature branch, (b) `git diff origin/main..HEAD -- 'crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs' | wc -l` returns > 200, (c) `git status --porcelain` is clean, (d) `git rev-parse HEAD` == `git rev-parse @{upstream}` (branch pushed), AND (e) `ucil-build/work-orders/0024-ready-for-review.md` exists. If any check fails, either keep working or write an escalation explaining the specific blocker. Silent session-exit on this WO is a catastrophic regression of the WO-0012/0020 pattern.

**Re-using WO-0012/0020's plan** — WO-0012's envelope + RCA are the source-of-truth for 'what to build'. WO-0024 is a fresh branch (`feat/WO-0024-kg-crud-and-hot-staging`) starting from clean `origin/main` to avoid inheriting stale state from either prior dead-end branch. Do NOT rebase/cherry-pick from WO-0012 or WO-0020 — the chrono dep commit on WO-0020 is redone as commit 1 here for a self-contained reviewable branch.

Module-root flat tests (no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005 — the frozen selector `knowledge_graph::` (F02) + `knowledge_graph::test_hot_staging_writes` (F08) are both path prefixes; placing tests flat mirrors `test_schema_creation` at `knowledge_graph.rs:472-474` AND the WO-0016 `quality_pipeline::test_*` AND the WO-0023 `call_hierarchy::test_*` (7 tests, all flat, all passed) recent precedent. This was the WO-0006 / WO-0007 regression mode and the RCA §Invariants line 1.

INIT_SQL stays byte-for-byte frozen (scope_out item) — all required tables + columns + UNIQUE constraints are already in place. `entities.UNIQUE(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from)` is at line 125, relations columns at 128-137, hot-staging tables at 190-216. Do NOT add columns or indexes. If a column is missing, STOP and write an ADR.

`upsert_entity` uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) DO UPDATE SET t_last_verified = datetime('now'), access_count = access_count + 1 RETURNING id` — the `RETURNING id` gives the caller the row id on both insert AND conflict branches in one round-trip. `test_entity_unique_constraint_updates` will flake if the conflict branch does not return id.

`get_entity_as_of` string comparison: `t_valid_from`/`t_valid_to` are TEXT columns in SQLite; pick ONE timestamp format (RFC-3339 via `chrono::DateTime<Utc>::to_rfc3339()`) and use it consistently. Do NOT mix `datetime('now')` (space separator) with RFC-3339 (T separator + offset) on the same column used in range queries.

All writers (`upsert_entity`, `upsert_relation`, `stage_hot_observation`, `stage_hot_convention_signal`, `stage_hot_architecture_delta`) MUST route through `execute_in_transaction` (the existing helper at `knowledge_graph.rs:430-440` uses `TransactionBehavior::Immediate`). Direct `self.conn.execute(...)` for writes is forbidden — the grep guard in `acceptance_criteria` will catch it. This mirrors WO-0016's `persist_diagnostics` pattern + WO-0023's `persist_call_hierarchy_*` pattern.

Reads (`get_entity_by_qualified_name`, `get_entity_as_of`, `list_entities_by_file`, `list_relations_by_source`) use `self.conn.prepare(...)?.query_row(...)` or `.query_map(...)` — no transaction needed.

`chrono` workspace dep MUST use `default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"]` — default-features pulls the `oldtime` / `time 0.1` transitive chain that emits clippy-deny warnings on some rustc versions (WO-0012 RCA §Risk point 5). Model after `uuid` at `Cargo.toml:70`.

Re-exports: `lib.rs` currently has `pub use knowledge_graph::{KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError};` — extend to `pub use knowledge_graph::{Entity, HotObservation, KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError, Relation, WalCheckpointMode};` (alphabetical so clippy-pedantic doesn't trip on ordering).

No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection` (phase-log invariant 1) — tests open a real tempfile-backed SQLite db via `tempfile::TempDir::new()` + `KnowledgeGraph::open(...)`. The `test_schema_creation` pattern at `knowledge_graph.rs:499-503` is the exemplar. WO-0016 `test_fixtures::open_kg()` and WO-0023's adaptation are also live precedents.

Clippy pedantic + nursery are in effect (inherited from `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs:7`) — expect `doc_markdown`, `missing_const_for_fn`, `missing_errors_doc`, `must_use_candidate`, `option_if_let_else` on the additions. Fix, don't blanket-allow.

Conventional Commits with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W4-F02` (or `-F08` on the hot-staging + WAL commits), `Work-order: WO-0024` trailers on every commit; push after every commit; no `--amend` after push; no force-push. Target 8 commits, ~530 lines total (matching WO-0020's commit plan budget).

Reality-check / mutation-oracle survival (cf. WO-0017 / WO-0018 / WO-0019 / WO-0023 pattern): `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F02` and `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W4-F08` MUST both pass on the delivered branch. Stashing the new CRUD + hot-staging code must cause the selectors to fail (via compile error or missing tests); popping must restore. If the script trips on a brand-new-module heuristic (WO-0014/0015/0016/0023 precedent; escalation `20260415-1630`), the verifier performs the manual two-step instead — document this possibility in the ready-for-review marker so the verifier doesn't re-flag.
Do NOT edit `verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md` — it is the RCA record used as context. Keep it as historical record alongside WO-0012's + WO-0020's envelopes.

commit_plan.subject: add chrono workspace dep (default-features off) + wire into ucil-core
knowledge_graph: add Entity + Relation + HotObservation + WalCheckpointMode with serde derives; re-export from lib.rs
knowledge_graph: upsert_entity + get_entity_by_qualified_name + list_entities_by_file + 3 module-root tests
